summary:
CURIO→KP INTEGRATION SURFACE — complete extraction + bidirectional wish-list (sources: wf-results/designs.json[1] core-plus-heads winner, wf-results/roadmap.json Phase 3, inbox/curio-production-plan-2026-07-03.html §6-7, rfc/0018, adr/0029, KP scaffold packages/shared/frontmatter-schema.md).

== (1) EVERYTHING CURIO EMITS THAT THE KP CAN CONSUME ==
A. frontmatter-v1 (vault export, core module export/): per-article Markdown note = sanitized HTML → htmd DOM-walk CommonMark, YAML frontmatter rendered from user-overridable minijinja template. Default fields: title, author, source (=source URL), feed, published, saved, tags, curio_id (UUIDv7, THE stable identity key), checksum (sha256 of normalized body — deliberately mirrors RFC-0018's checksum-for-dedupe/incremental-reindex key), schema_version. Semantics: promotion always targets a NAMED destination (raw paths never cross IPC/CLI); atomic tmp+rename writes (vault expected git-/watcher-observed mid-write); deterministic slug-collision suffixes; path-escape-proof templates (property-tested); per-feed/per-tag routing to subdirectories; optional vault-relative image assets; re-promotion updates in place keyed on curio_id. Both desktop one-keystroke promote and `curio export` produce identical output.
B. .curio/manifest.json (in-vault, dual-homed with a SQLite table): curio_id → vault-relative path + last checksum. Enables idempotency (same content → zero writes), rename tracking, and wipe-and-reinstall reconciliation (fresh Curio pointed at existing vault reconciles, never duplicates — CI-gated acceptance). The manifest is Curio-owned state living INSIDE the KP vault — it must be committed/synced for the reconcile story to work.
C. events-v1 (core module events/): append-only event-sourced state; JSONL projection {schema_version, event: subscribed|read|starred|read_later|promoted, article:{curio_id,url,title,feed}, ts}. Sinks: rotating cursor-checkpointed file sink (explicitly "pointable at a synced folder or the vault's .curio/events/") AND a webhook sink (push, named for "the KP librarian"). Cursor semantics (`events since <cursor>`) designed in Phase 2 so CLI tail, JSONL export, and a future server-head GET /v1/events?since= share one cursor.
D. curio-cli v1 surface: add (subscribe feed) / fetch / query / search / export / events tail / events since <cursor> / opml import+export / `export --all` (full portability archive: OPML + events JSONL + settings + read-later — a KP backup/reseed primitive). NOTE: there is NO inbound article-level surface in v1 — no save-arbitrary-URL, no read-later-injection verb. `add` takes feeds only.
E. Published contracts: curio-types (schemars) → semver'd JSON Schema artifacts (frontmatter-v1, events-v1) per release + human-readable docs/schemas/frontmatter-v1.md and events-v1.md with a written breaking-change policy (additive in minors, breaking in majors) + a MANDATORY schema-review gate before first external consumer wiring — the KP is explicitly named consumer #1, and first-30-days item 10 says "wire the Knowledge Plane as external consumer #1... configuration only".
F. Extended OPML (curio:* namespace round-trip) — subscription state as interchange.
G. Post-v1 (designed, not shipped): git-push vault destination kind ("for the KP it pushes promoted notes straight into the git-mirrored inbox/"); highlights-in-vault (annotations as vault file edits, never app DB — RFC-0018's canonical/derived rule by construction); MCP server head over curio-core; curio-server head (axum GReader+Fever+REST, "could ride the G11 ingress tier"); local trainable per-feed ranking (NewsBlur-style, inspectable features); file-based journal merge (per-device ULID event journals in a synced folder) as the named serverless multi-device direction.
Acceptance proof of genericity (Phase 3): "a standalone script with no Curio code ingests the exported vault + tails the JSONL and reconstructs read/star state — run against a scratch KP-shaped inbox/".

== (2) WHAT "NATIVE" KP-SIDE CONSUMPTION REQUIRES ==
1. Schema pinning + validation: KP vendors pinned copies of frontmatter-v1/events-v1 JSON Schemas (packages/shared), validates every Curio-sourced note and every JSONL line in CI and at ingest; tolerates additive minors, hard-fails majors. Requires Curio to expose stable schema $id/URLs (wish-list item below — currently "published per release" without a stated registry location).
2. Identity + incremental reindex: curio_id (UUIDv7) is the identity key; checksum is the reindex key — the KP watcher re-embeds only chunks whose checksum changed, exactly RFC-0018 §Index's rule. The mirror is deliberate; the KP ingest adapter should key its index records on curio_id, not path (renames are manifest-tracked).
3. Field-mapping adapter, NOT template divergence: KP's own frontmatter schema (packages/shared/frontmatter-schema.md) COLLIDES with frontmatter-v1 on `source` (KP: enum zotero|rss|web|manual; Curio: source URL) and uses different names (url/date_added/status vs source/saved). The distribution requirement (OSS KP must accept VANILLA Curio) forces the resolution into a KP-side adapter (a `curio` source kind in packages/ingest that maps frontmatter-v1 → KP index records), never into a custom Curio template on Alex's instance — a custom template would break frontmatter-v1 validation and make Alex's install non-representative of the OSS story.
4. Event-cursor checkpointing: KP interest-model ingester persists the Curio cursor per sink, consumes file-sink JSONL (rotation-aware) and/or receives webhook pushes; replay-idempotent because events are append-only.
5. Manifest awareness / write-ownership rule: any note whose path appears in .curio/manifest.json is Curio-owned. Critical failure mode: Curio re-promotion re-renders the ENTIRE file from template when article content changes — any KP-added frontmatter (e.g. status: unprocessed) or in-body edit is silently clobbered. Therefore: (a) the KP NEVER edits Curio-owned notes (no status field injection — Curio-note lifecycle state lives index-side or in the adapter); (b) librarian enrichment lands in KP-owned companion/literature notes that wikilink the Curio note (zero Curio changes, works day 1); (c) in-note enrichment waits on a managed-region contract (wish-list). KP watcher must also debounce on tmp+rename and ignore .curio/ temp files; git hooks/normalizers in the vault must exempt manifest-listed notes.
6. Deliver the schema review: Curio's Phase-3 schema-review gate is load-bearing and the KP is the reviewer. Concrete findings to file now: (i) the winning events enum has NO negation events (read_later removal, unstar, mark-unread) — the server-first variant had read_later_added/read_later_removed; without negations, state reconstruction is monotone-only and interest-model decay signals are lost; (ii) article payload {curio_id,url,title,feed} lacks tags — the interest model wants topic signals at event time; (iii) ts format/ordering guarantees and cursor opacity need specification.

== (3) BIDIRECTIONAL OPPORTUNITIES — depth vs complexity ==
R1. Librarian digest as a local Atom/RSS feed Curio subscribes to — DEPTH: HIGH (the reading surface displays librarian output; the discovery loop closes inside the app Alex already reads in; digest items → read/star/promote → events → interest model = full feedback circle). COMPLEXITY: LOW KP-side (librarian emits digest.xml with stable per-digest GUIDs; static file served by G11 caddy per ADR-0029's always-on tier). BLOCKER: Curio v1's mandatory PolicedClient deny-resolves loopback/RFC1918/link-local on ALL outbound fetches with no documented override — Curio literally cannot subscribe to a LAN feed. Requires wish-list W1 (per-feed private-network allowlist). VERDICT: build it; the enabling Curio change is tiny and security-scoped.
R2. now.md as shared steering state — DEPTH: MEDIUM. now.md is the discovery loop's input (Phase 5). Curio reading it directly in v1: NO (no such feature, and coupling Curio to a KP file inverts the generic-contract posture). Right shape: now.md stays KP-internal; its influence reaches Curio via R1 (digest content) and later via R5 (ranking hints). VERDICT: no Curio change; do not create a Curio→now.md dependency.
R3. Librarian enriching Curio-promoted notes (zotero backlinks, related-claims sections) via proposal branches — DEPTH: HIGH. Two variants: (a) companion literature notes wikilinking the Curio note — zero Curio changes, honors RFC-0018's proposals/-branch rule, safe under re-promotion clobbering: SHIP THIS FIRST; (b) in-note appended sections — requires the managed-region contract (W2) so Curio re-export preserves content after a curio-owned-region marker; COMPLEXITY: MEDIUM (changes Curio's checksum semantics to cover only the managed region). VERDICT: (a) now, (b) post-v1 with the marker RESERVED in frontmatter-v1.md docs from day 1 so existing notes stay forward-compatible.
R4. KP-driven read-later injection — Curio has NO inbound surface for it in v1 (checked: CLI = add/fetch/query/search/export/events/opml; add is feeds-only; there is also no save-arbitrary-URL primitive at all). DEPTH: MEDIUM (librarian pushes a found paper into Alex's queue), COMPLEXITY: MEDIUM (needs both an ad-hoc-URL ingest path and a new CLI verb, e.g. `curio later <url>`; also natural as a server-head endpoint later). VERDICT: post-v1 wish-list (W5); R1's digest feed captures ~80% of the value at zero Curio cost meanwhile (digest item + one keystroke = read-later).
R5. Interest-model feedback ranking the Curio triage view — the roadmap already lists post-v1 "local trainable per-feed ranking (inspectable features)". DEPTH: HIGH long-term, COMPLEXITY: HIGH and premature. VERDICT: flag as a named FUTURE contract (ranking-hints input — e.g. a hints file/endpoint the ranker treats as one inspectable feature); design placeholder only, no v1 ask.
R6. Events webhook → librarian maintenance-loop trigger — v1 webhook sink pointed at a small KP trigger endpoint on G11; promoted events wake the inbox-drain instead of polling. DEPTH: MEDIUM, COMPLEXITY: LOW, zero Curio changes. SHIP.
R7. OPML round-trip into the vault (`curio opml export` → vault raw/, versioned) — makes subscription state canonical-in-git, keeps Curio's DB fully disposable. Zero Curio changes. SHIP as a cron/agent habit.
R8. `curio export --all` as the KP's Curio-reseed/backup primitive; MCP-server-head (post-v1) as the eventual agents-drive-the-reader surface federated behind the KP retrieval MCP — both zero-cost to note now.

== (4) WHAT THE CURIO PLAN SHOULD ADD (contract wish-list, ranked) ==
W1. Per-feed private-network/loopback allowlist opt-in in PolicedClient (explicit per-feed flag, default deny stays) — small, should land in v1/Phase 2; without it the digest-feed loop (R1) is impossible and every self-hoster consuming any local feed is blocked.
W2. Managed-region re-export semantics: reserve a curio-owned-region marker in frontmatter-v1.md NOW (docs-only in v1); post-v1, scope re-export rewrites + checksum to the region, preserving trailing user/librarian content — the same contract highlights-in-vault already needs.
W3. Schema-registry convention: stable $id URLs for frontmatter-v1/events-v1 JSON Schemas (e.g. <domain>/schemas/frontmatter/v1.json), a per-schema CHANGELOG, and machine-fetchable location documented in the integration guide — pinning target for the KP and every other consumer.
W4. Events-v1 completeness (rides the existing Phase-3 schema-review gate; KP files the review): negation events (read_later_removed/unstarred/unread) or documented monotone semantics; tags in the article payload; ts format + ordering + cursor-opacity spec.
W5. Inbound verb `curio later <url>` (ad-hoc URL → read-later) — post-v1; the librarian→reader push seam and the future server-head endpoint in one contract.
W6. Digest-feed convention documented in the vault/events integration guide (subscribing Curio to a locally-published agent digest; GUID stability; pairs with W1) — turns R1 into a supported OSS pattern (any agent, not just the KP librarian).

== TOPOLOGY (no ADR-0029 amendment needed) ==
Curio desktop runs on Alex's Mac writing directly into the local KP-vault clone (canonical stays on the Mac, git mirrors to G11 — same doctrine as the management plane); the webhook target + digest feed serving live on pve-net/G11 (always-on tier, RFC-0017/ADR-0029 posture); interest-model/index consumption on pve. Post-v1 curio-server, if built, fronts couch/mobile behind the G11 ingress tier — already named in the plan. Timeline: Curio contracts ship at Phase 3 (~11–14.5 weeks from Curio start); KP integration thereafter is configuration only, but the KP schema review (W4) and the ingest-adapter + manifest-ownership design can and should be done now.
facts: frontmatter-v1 default fields: title, author, source (URL), feed, published, saved, tags, curio_id (UUIDv7), checksum (sha256 of normalized body), schema_version — rendered from user-overridable minijinja templates; checksum deliberately mirrors RFC-0018's checksum-for-dedupe/incremental-reindex key (designs.json[1].kp_seam; roadmap.json Phase 3)

events-v1 JSONL line: {schema_version, event: subscribed|read|starred|read_later|promoted, article:{curio_id,url,title,feed}, ts}; sinks = rotating cursor-checkpointed file sink (pointable at a synced folder or the vault's .curio/events/) + webhook sink named as push-capable 'for the KP librarian'; one cursor shared by `events tail`, `events since <cursor>`, and a future server-head GET /v1/events?since= (roadmap.json Phase 2+3 deliverables)

The winning (core-plus-heads) events enum has NO negation events — the losing server-first design had read_later_added/read_later_removed (designs.json[2].kp_seam vs designs.json[1].kp_seam); state reconstruction from the winner's stream is monotone-only

.curio/manifest.json is dual-homed (SQLite table + in-vault file), maps curio_id → vault-relative path + last checksum; wipe-and-reinstall reconciliation against it is a CI-gated Phase 3 acceptance criterion, so the manifest must live committed inside the vault (roadmap.json Phase 3)

Promotion is always to a NAMED destination — raw filesystem paths never cross IPC or CLI flags; writes are atomic tmp+rename because the vault is expected git-/watcher-observed mid-write (designs.json[1] key_decisions[6], roadmap Phase 3 'named destination registry')

curio-cli v1 surface: add / fetch / query / search / export / events tail / events since / opml import+export / export --all (full-portability archive: OPML + events JSONL + settings + read-later); `add` subscribes FEEDS only — no inbound article-level or save-arbitrary-URL surface exists in v1 (roadmap.json Phase 3 deliverables)

Curio v1's mandatory PolicedClient deny-resolves loopback/RFC1918/link-local on ALL outbound requests with no documented per-feed override (roadmap.json Phase 2 fetch/ deliverable) — Curio v1 cannot subscribe to a LAN-served librarian digest feed without a new allowlist feature

Curio re-promotion updates the note in place when article content changes (re-rendered from template, keyed on curio_id via manifest) — any KP-added frontmatter or in-body edits to a Curio-owned note get clobbered; safe librarian enrichment = companion notes, until a managed-region contract exists (designs.json[1].kp_seam idempotency semantics)

Both schemas ship as semver'd JSON Schema artifacts from curio-types (schemars) per release plus human-readable frontmatter-v1.md/events-v1.md with a written breaking-change policy (additive minors, breaking majors), and a MANDATORY schema-review gate before first external consumer wiring — the KP is named external consumer #1, wired in the first 30 days as 'configuration only' (roadmap.json Phase 3 + first_30_days)

Post-v1 (designed, unshipped): git-push vault destination ('pushes promoted notes straight into the git-mirrored inbox/'), highlights-in-vault as file edits (never app DB — RFC-0018's canonical/derived rule by construction), MCP server head over curio-core, curio-server (GReader+Fever, 'could ride the G11 ingress tier'), local trainable per-feed ranking with inspectable features, file-based journal merge (roadmap.json post_v1)

Phase 3 acceptance includes a generic-consumer proof: a standalone no-Curio-code script ingests the exported vault + tails the JSONL and reconstructs read/star state, demoed against a KP-shaped scratch inbox/ (roadmap.json Phase 3 acceptance)

KP's own frontmatter schema (knowledge-plane/packages/shared/frontmatter-schema.md) defines source as an enum (zotero|rss|web|manual) plus url/date_added/status/checksum/zotero_key — it collides with frontmatter-v1 on the `source` key (Curio: source = URL) and has no curio source kind; a KP-side ingest adapter is required, and the OSS-distributability requirement forbids resolving this by customizing Alex's Curio template away from vanilla frontmatter-v1

Curio's annotations/highlights are specified to land in the vault as file edits, never app-private DB state — Curio's SQLite is derived/disposable under the KP canonical/derived rule; promoted notes carry everything downstream needs (designs.json[1].kp_seam; brief §6)
Curio timeline: contracts ship at end of Phase 3, cumulatively ~11–14.5 weeks from start (P0 1.5w + P1 2.5w overlap + P2 6–8w + P3 3–4w); v1.0 total ~28 weeks (roadmap.json sizes)

Publishing the schemas freezes them immediately because the KP becomes a real downstream — 'a v0 mistake (e.g. wrong identity key) is a breaking change with a real downstream; schema review before first KP wiring is load-bearing' (designs.json[1].risks)

ADR-0029: MCP servers + ingress live on pve-net/G11 (always-on tier); OCR/embedding/indices/librarian on pve; pve-ha excluded; vault MCP reads a git-mirrored vault copy off disk (no live Obsidian app); Zotero attachments via self-hosted WebDAV on CT 206 (192.0.2.15), zotero.org sync keeps metadata
open_questions: Where do the events JSONL files land under the canonical/derived rule — committed in-vault (.curio/events/, versioned reading history as canonical-ish hand-generated data) or in a derived data dir outside git? The plan supports both; the KP must pick one and encode it in the integration config.

How does the KP track lifecycle state (unprocessed→linked→promoted) for Curio-owned notes, given it must never write a status field into them (re-promotion clobbers)? Leading answer: index-side state keyed on curio_id — needs a decision in the KP ingest design.

Schema-review outcomes (KP is the reviewer, review should be drafted NOW even though Curio Phase 3 is months out): negation events yes/no, tags in event payload, ts format + ordering guarantees, cursor opacity/format — who files this review and where does it live (KP repo docs/ vs Curio issue)?

Does Alex's Curio vault destination point at the KP vault's inbox/ directly, or at a curio/ subdirectory inbox routes from? (frontmatter-v1's per-feed/per-tag routing supports either; affects the manifest-ownership boundary and inbox-drain logic.)

Which component hosts the Curio webhook target on G11 — a new tiny trigger shim CT/service, or an existing guest (push-notifier? caddy?) — and does the librarian's maintenance loop go event-driven (webhook) or stay polling with the file sink as source of truth?
Curio name/trademark clearance (Zengobi collision) is unresolved and Phase-0-blocking on the Curio side — the KP's public integration docs can't name the reader until that settles.

Post-v1 sync direction (server head on G11 vs client mode vs journal merge) affects whether the KP's couch/mobile reading story ever needs KP-side work — deliberately deferred, but the KP OSS docs should describe the integration in reader-agnostic contract terms (frontmatter-v1/events-v1) so any conforming producer plugs in.
stale_or_wrong: RFC-0018 §Ingestion/§Rollout Phase 2 specifies the KP builds its own 'RSS fetch' into inbox/ — with Curio as the designated reading surface, KP-side RSS fetching is a duplicate pipeline; the anticipated KP-integration RFC amendment should re-scope Phase 2 ingestion to Zotero/web-clip only, with RSS entering via Curio promotion (frontmatter-v1). Building both would create two RSS fetchers with divergent normalization.

knowledge-plane scaffold .mcp.json still says both MCP servers run client-side on Alex's Mac and that the Obsidian MCP 'requires the Obsidian desktop app running — not headless' — contradicts ADR-0029's amendment (MCPs on G11; vault/retrieval MCP reads a git-mirrored copy, no live app). Stale scaffold comment.

RFC-0018 §Index claims full-text/BM25 is 'free via the Obsidian REST plugin/Omnisearch' — that reintroduces the live-Obsidian-desktop dependency ADR-0029 explicitly removed, and violates the new directive's 'Obsidian is recommended viewer, never a hard dependency' distributability constraint; BM25 must come from the KP's own index (SQLite FTS5/tantivy/Postgres FTS).

KP packages/shared/frontmatter-schema.md is underdesigned for Curio: no `curio` source kind, `source` key semantics collide with frontmatter-v1 (enum vs URL), no curio_id field, and its `status` field cannot be stamped onto Curio-owned notes without being clobbered on re-promotion — needs the adapter/mapping design, not a drive-by field addition.

The Curio plan's events-v1 enum (subscribed|read|starred|read_later|promoted) silently dropped the removal events present in the server-first design (read_later_added/read_later_removed) — as written, the Phase-3 acceptance 'reconstructs read/star state' only holds for monotone histories; this is exactly the class of defect the plan's own schema-review gate exists to catch, and the KP (consumer #1) should file it.

The Curio plan's PolicedClient (deny loopback/RFC1918 on all fetches, no per-feed override) forecloses the highest-value zero-code integration (Curio subscribing to a locally served librarian digest feed) — the plan should add an explicit per-feed private-network allowlist (W1); currently unaddressed anywhere in designs.json/roadmap.json/the brief.
